content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-high

## Summary

Accept all four amendments. Three identify concrete correctness/robustness issues in prompt rendering and contract propagation, and one identifies a real data-model wiring gap that leaves part of the contract permanently nonfunctional in production.

## Votes

- `fr-1-617368af`: Accept
  The guidance text currently names the contract as `bead_execution_prompt/1.` rather than `bead_execution_prompt/1`. Even if today this string is only instructional, it is still an incorrect identifier and can mislead LLM consumers or any future text-based extraction logic. Fixing the punctuation is a low-risk correctness improvement.
- `fr-1-74a3f619`: Accept
  `already_planned_elsewhere` is effectively dead in production: the only real constructor hardcodes it to empty, and there is no upstream data source to populate it. That means a canonical contract section can never carry real project data, which is a maintainability and product-correctness gap. Wiring it or explicitly documenting it as a placeholder would make the behavior intentional instead of silently inert.
- `fr-1-60ee64b1`: Accept
  This identifies a real end-to-end contract integrity problem. The contract marker is detected by exact string match, but prompt review rewrites `prompt.md` through generic templates and then persists the rewritten prompt, with no preservation rule for the marker or section semantics. That can silently disable later contract-aware behavior. Extending the contract handling so prompt-review and downstream judging stages preserve and respect the canonical bead contract is the correct robustness fix.
- `fr-1-1646d5c1`: Accept
  Wrapping free-form `agents_guidance` in a synthesized `- ...` bullet is unsafe for markdown content. Multi-line text, headings, fenced code, and nested lists can be malformed or semantically altered, which is especially bad because this section carries repo-specific execution instructions. Preserving the guidance verbatim is the safer and more maintainable rendering behavior.
